column8: Mutant fails on import. LLM acknowledges this, then generates a test with an error, then fixes it first try.
Mutant fails on import. LLM fails to import the mutant, only trying a wrong path. Then recreates the mutant instead.
Mutant fails on import. LLM acknowledges this, then generates a test that is wrong but accidentally works.
Mutant fails on import. LLM only generates tests that fail on the correct version too.
Mutant fails on import. LLM takes a couple of experiments to realize the imported "PRETTIFY_RE" is a dict of regexes and not the regex, but it makes its way there.
Mutant fails on import. LLM fails to import the mutant, only trying a wrong path. Then recreates the mutant instead.
LLM first hypothesizes that the mutant will fail on import, the hypothesizes about parsing different HTML tags, trying different outputs each time. Very nice!
LLM fails to construct an error-free test case, trying more inputs and more complex inputs with each attempt (which is not helping).

LLM first hypothesizes that the mutant will fail on import, then hypothesizes about parsing multiline HTML tags, which is the right idea. It takes a couple of experiments to get there, because it omits the imports in two of them. Then takes some attempts to convert the experiment into a test. The LLM actually interleaves experiments and tests here.
LLM doesn't find the inputs, trying more or less the same ones (with minor changes) each attempt.
LLM first hypothesizes that the mutant will fail on import, then tries a complex JSON input and creates the test.
LLM fails to construct an error-free test case, trying more inputs and more complex inputs with each attempt (which is not helping, again).
LLM first fails to import correcly. Then messes up the test by adding unnecessary calls to the target function that throw exceptions for both versions.
